data.title: High Tide Launches Probe Into Potential Manipulation of Its Shares
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An update from High Tide ( (TSE:HITI) ) is now available.

On April 23, 2026, High Tide said it is examining unusual trading patterns in its common shares after more than ten quarters of earnings that typically beat analyst expectations but are followed by next-day price declines despite positive after-hours reactions. Management and the board, working with the company’s market maker, have been monitoring trading data around earnings and other major disclosure events to determine whether activity reflects normal market dynamics.

The company believes there are reasonable grounds to suspect some trading may be inconsistent with ordinary market forces and potentially contrary to securities laws in Canada and the U.S., and it plans to hire forensic and market-structure experts to conduct an independent review. High Tide intends to share the investigation’s findings with relevant regulators and fully cooperate with any inquiries, while inviting shareholders and market participants to confidentially provide information, signaling a bid to protect investors and reinforce confidence in the integrity of its stock trading.

The most recent analyst rating on (TSE:HITI) stock is a Buy with a C$7.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on High Tide stock, see the TSE:HITI Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on HITI Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, HITI is a Neutral.

The score is held back primarily by weak financial quality (persistent losses and elevated leverage) and bearish technicals (price below major moving averages with negative MACD). Offsetting these, cash flow has improved meaningfully and the latest earnings call was constructive with stronger adjusted EBITDA/free-cash-flow trends and clear growth initiatives, but valuation remains relatively expensive given current profitability.

To see Spark’s full report on HITI stock, click here.

More about High Tide

High Tide Inc. is a Calgary-based, retail-forward cannabis enterprise with diversified, vertically integrated operations spanning brick-and-mortar stores, e-commerce accessories, and hemp-derived CBD products. Its Canna Cabana chain is the largest cannabis retailer in Canada with 221 domestic outlets plus one international store, a roughly 12% national market share, and a recent brick-and-mortar expansion into Germany via medical distributor Remexian Pharma.

Through Remexian Pharma in Germany, High Tide also imports and wholesales medical cannabis from 19 countries, broadening its international footprint beyond recreational retail. The company has been repeatedly recognized for rapid growth, including listings among Canada’s Top Growing Companies and the Financial Times’ Americas’ Fastest Growing Companies, underscoring its positioning as a leading cannabis retailer and distributor in North America and Europe.

Average Trading Volume: 72,919

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: C$287.4M

For a thorough assessment of HITI stock, go to TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

CS Disco ( (LAW) ) has issued an update.

On April 22, 2026, CS Disco, Inc. expanded its board of directors from nine to ten members and appointed Toby Williams, president and chief executive of Paylocity Holding Corporation, as an independent Class II director with a term running until the 2026 annual meeting. Williams brings extensive experience in cloud-based human capital management, higher education software, and corporate development, and he will receive equity-based compensation and a cash retainer under CS Disco’s standard non-employee director compensation policy.

There are no related-party arrangements or family relationships associated with Williams’s appointment, and he has entered into CS Disco’s customary indemnification agreement for directors under Delaware law. The company also disclosed that current directors Colette Pierce Burnette and Aaron Clark will not stand for reelection at the 2026 annual meeting, at which point the board size will be reduced from ten to eight members, signaling a planned reshaping of board composition.

The most recent analyst rating on (LAW) stock is a Buy with a $10.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on CS Disco stock, see the LAW Stock Forecast page.

The earnings call adds some support via accelerating software growth and an improving adjusted EBITDA trend with a stated breakeven path, while technicals are mixed (short-term strength but longer-term downtrend). Valuation is constrained by a negative P/E and no dividend yield provided.

An announcement from Frontier Nuclear and Minerals ( (FNUC) ) is now available.

On April 23, 2026, Frontier Nuclear and Minerals reported that its portfolio company Kadmos Energy Services is preparing to launch a dual experimental validation program for its small modular reactor design at an engineering facility in Idaho Falls. The move deepens Frontier’s exposure to the SMR segment and is positioned as a strategic step toward turning Kadmos’s technology into a licensable, commercially deployable asset serving high-demand users.

Kadmos’s Idaho Falls facility is set up to generate experimental data to verify and validate thermal-hydraulic models, supporting design development and future regulatory submissions while leveraging proximity to Idaho National Laboratory. By basing its SMR on proven light water reactor technology and targeting customers such as data centers, industrial users and defense-related applications, Kadmos aims to reduce licensing risk and time to market, reinforcing Frontier’s broader strategy to capture value across the nuclear fuel cycle.

More about Frontier Nuclear and Minerals

Frontier Nuclear and Minerals Inc., doing business as Frontier Nuclear, is a U.S.-focused nuclear fuel cycle company listed on Nasdaq under the ticker FNUC. It is building a nuclear fuel platform through uranium exploration and development, alongside targeted investments in enrichment and reactor technologies, including small modular reactors via portfolio companies such as Kadmos Energy Services.

data.title: PMGC Subsidiary Secures Exclusive Option for GPS-Denied Drone Navigation
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An announcement from PMGC Holdings ( (ELAB) ) is now available.

On April 23, 2026, NorthStrive Defense Tech LLC announced it had secured an exclusive license option over a U.S.-patented cooperative navigation system designed for drones and unmanned aircraft systems operating in GPS-jammed, spoofed, or denied environments. The option, which covers exclusive rights to key patent claims and non-exclusive access to related know-how in the aerospace and defense field, marks NorthStrive’s second contract since its formation and potentially broadens its emerging portfolio in advanced autonomous and counter‑UAS technologies.

The technology uses visual-inertial odometry, sensor fusion, and cooperative multi-vehicle data sharing to enable accurate navigation without GPS, addressing a capability gap identified by the U.S. Department of Defense and NATO allies as drone warfare and electronic warfare intensify. By targeting defense, counter-drone, electronic warfare, and select commercial applications such as urban delivery and industrial automation, the option positions PMGC’s subsidiary to tap into a drone navigation market projected for rapid growth, though the company cautioned there is no assurance a definitive license will be completed or the system successfully commercialized.

Spark’s Take on ELAB Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, ELAB is a Neutral.

The score is held down primarily by weak financial performance (losses, volatile revenue, and sustained cash burn) and bearish technicals (price below key moving averages with negative MACD). Corporate updates provide some offset via new long-term contracts and strategic progress, but financing-related dilution risk and limited valuation support keep the overall score low.

To see Spark’s full report on ELAB stock, click here.

More about PMGC Holdings

PMGC Holdings Inc. is a diversified holding company that grows its portfolio through strategic acquisitions, investments, and development across multiple industries. Its wholly owned subsidiary NorthStrive Defense Tech LLC focuses on identifying, acquiring, and commercializing advanced defense technologies, with an emphasis on drone and autonomous systems for aerospace and defense markets.

NorthStrive Defense Tech LLC operates as PMGC’s defense-tech platform, targeting opportunities in unmanned aircraft systems, counter-drone solutions, and autonomous capabilities. The subsidiary aims to position itself within fast-growing segments such as GPS-denied navigation, electronic warfare support technologies, and defense modernization programs aligned with U.S. and allied military needs.

data.title: S&T Bancorp Reports Strong Q1 2026 Earnings Growth
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

S&T Bancorp ( (STBA) ) just unveiled an announcement.

On April 23, 2026, S&T Bancorp, Inc., the Indiana, Pa.-based holding company for S&T Bank, reported first-quarter 2026 net income of $35.1 million, up from both the prior quarter and the year-earlier period, with diluted EPS rising to $0.94. The quarter featured strong profitability metrics, including a 1.44% return on average assets and 9.77% return on average equity, alongside robust capital levels that remained above well-capitalized regulatory thresholds.

Operationally, S&T posted a solid net interest margin of 3.92% despite slightly lower net interest income tied to fewer days in the quarter and the absence of a prior-period interest recovery. Deposit growth was a major highlight, with total deposits rising $226.4 million, driven by $306.5 million in customer deposit inflows and reduced reliance on brokered deposits, while total portfolio loans fell $112.6 million amid lower fundings, reduced utilization and higher commercial real estate payoffs.

Asset quality improved, as net charge-offs fell sharply to $1.7 million, or 0.09% of average loans, compared with $11.0 million, or 0.54%, in the fourth quarter of 2025, and nonperforming assets declined to $49.9 million, or 0.63% of total loans plus OREO. The allowance for credit losses held steady at $93.3 million, or 1.17% of total portfolio loans, and management described overall NPA levels as manageable.

Noninterest income slipped to $13.6 million on seasonally softer customer activity, particularly in debit and credit card fees, while noninterest expense eased to $56.7 million, helped by lower salaries and medical costs. Total assets were unchanged at $9.9 billion compared with year-end 2025, but the balance sheet mix shifted toward higher cash and deposits and lower loans and borrowings, reflecting strong funding inflows.

S&T also continued to deploy capital to shareholders, repurchasing 1,146,100 shares for $49.6 million in the first quarter of 2026, bringing two-quarter buybacks to 2,094,370 shares totaling $85.8 million under its existing program. Management characterized the quarter’s results as evidence of strong earnings performance, solid returns and momentum in relationship-based deposit growth, reinforcing its strategic focus on customers, communities and shareholders in its core Pennsylvania and Ohio markets.

The most recent analyst rating on (STBA) stock is a Hold with a $47.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on S&T Bancorp stock, see the STBA Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on STBA Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, STBA is a Outperform.

STBA scores well on solid financial performance and constructive earnings-call outlook (stable NIM, loan growth guidance, and continued buybacks), supported by attractive valuation (low P/E and ~3.3% yield). Technicals are positive but somewhat stretched, and credit metrics (charge-offs/NPAs and lower ACL) temper the upside.

To see Spark’s full report on STBA stock, click here.

More about S&T Bancorp

S&T Bancorp, Inc. is a $9.9 billion bank holding company headquartered in Indiana, Pennsylvania, and trades on the NASDAQ Global Select Market under the symbol STBA. Its principal subsidiary, S&T Bank, founded in 1902, operates in Pennsylvania and Ohio, providing a range of banking services to retail and commercial customers in those regional markets.

The company focuses on core relationship-based banking, including commercial and consumer lending, deposit products and related financial services, positioning itself as a community-oriented institution. Its operations emphasize stable asset quality, diversified funding sources and maintaining strong regulatory capital ratios to support growth and shareholder returns.

S&T Bancorp’s business model combines traditional banking with disciplined balance sheet management, seeking to optimize net interest margin while managing credit risk. The bank also uses share repurchase programs as part of its capital management strategy, reflecting an ongoing focus on shareholder value in a competitive regional banking landscape.

Average Trading Volume: 258,670

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: $1.59B

On April 23, 2026, Dragonfly Energy Holdings Corp. announced it received an allowance from the Japan Patent Office for its patent application titled “Powderized Solid-State Electrolyte and Electroactive Materials,” marking the company’s first patent application allowance in Japan. The latest announcement is out from PulteGroup ( (PHM) ).

PulteGroup reported first quarter 2026 results on April 23, with net income falling to $347 million, or $1.79 per share, from $523 million, or $2.57 per share, a year earlier as home sale revenues declined 12% to $3.3 billion. The builder saw a 7% drop in closing volumes and a 5% decrease in average selling price, while gross margin narrowed to 24.4% amid higher incentives used to address competitive pressures and clear excess spec inventory.

Despite softer earnings, net new orders rose 3% to 8,034 homes worth $4.6 billion, and community count increased 9%, lifting quarter-end backlog to 10,427 homes valued at $6.5 billion, highlighting underlying demand for homeownership despite affordability concerns. PulteGroup invested $1.3 billion in land acquisition and development, repurchased $308 million of stock, and its board approved a $1.5 billion increase in share repurchase authorization to $2.1 billion remaining, signaling confidence in long-term prospects and continued shareholder returns supported by a low 12.3% debt-to-capital ratio and $1.8 billion in cash.

West Bancorporation ( (WTBA) ) has provided an announcement.

West Bancorporation, Inc., headquartered in West Des Moines, Iowa, is the parent of community bank West Bank, which has served customers since 1893. The bank focuses on lending, deposit and trust services for small- to medium-sized businesses and consumers, operating branches in the Des Moines and Coralville markets in Iowa and four cities in Minnesota, including Rochester and St. Cloud.

The company’s regional community banking model positions it to compete for relationship-based commercial and retail clients. Its franchise emphasizes balance-sheet strength, credit quality and core deposit gathering across its Midwestern footprint, supporting stable earnings and consistent capital returns to shareholders through regular dividends.

West Bancorporation, Inc. reported first quarter 2026 net income of $10.6 million, or $0.61 per diluted share, up from $7.4 million in the fourth quarter of 2025 and $7.8 million a year earlier, reflecting a 34.8 percent year-over-year profit increase as net interest margin expanded. On April 22, 2026, the board declared a regular quarterly dividend of $0.25 per share, payable May 20, 2026 to shareholders of record on May 6, 2026.

The first quarter showed modest loan contraction driven by payoffs and refinancings, but credit quality remained exceptionally strong with no nonaccrual loans and lower watch list balances, while deposits fell sequentially due to normal cash flow fluctuations. Improved net interest margin, a lower cost of deposits, a better efficiency ratio and a higher tangible common equity ratio underpinned stronger profitability and capital, reinforcing the bank’s conservative risk profile and supporting its ability to sustain shareholder returns.

data.title: Arvana Finalizes Court-Approved Stock Settlement Agreement
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Arvana ( (AVNI) ) just unveiled an announcement.

On January 13, 2026, Arvana, Inc. entered into a settlement agreement with J.P. Carey Enterprises, Inc. to resolve $188,379.32 in past-due obligations that JPCarey had acquired and was litigating in Florida state court. The company agreed to satisfy the claim by issuing common stock in multiple tranches under a court-approved, registration-exempt structure, including an extra 250,000 shares to cover JPCarey’s legal fees, while capping JPCarey’s beneficial ownership at 4.99% of outstanding shares.

Following a fairness hearing on April 7, 2026, the court on April 11, 2026, approved the settlement and authorized Arvana to issue the shares without restrictive legends through electronic systems, contingent on standard transfer-agent requirements. The deal obliges Arvana to maintain sufficient authorized share capital and avoid impairing its stock’s trading status, with potential default and renewed litigation if it fails to deliver shares or faces trading suspensions or insolvency, while providing mutual releases once all shares are issued.

More about Arvana

Arvana, Inc. operates as a publicly traded company with its common stock subject to market-based pricing and trading limitations. The firm’s capital structure allows for the issuance of additional common shares, including in connection with legal settlements and other corporate obligations.

data.description:

Clean Energy Technologies ( (CETY) ) has provided an announcement.

On April 17, 2026, Clean Energy Technologies, Inc. disclosed it had received a notice from Nasdaq stating the company was not in compliance with listing rules because it had not yet filed its Form 10-K for the year ended December 31, 2025. The notice does not immediately affect the listing or trading of its securities, and the company is working to complete and file the overdue annual report.

Under Nasdaq procedures, Clean Energy Technologies has 60 days from receipt of the notice to submit a plan to regain compliance, with a potential extension of up to 180 days from the original Form 10-K due date if that plan is accepted. The company warned that a potential delisting could reduce stock liquidity and price, constrain access to public capital markets, limit investor interest and impair its ability to offer equity incentives to employees.

Wallbox ( (WBX) ) has provided an update.

Wallbox N.V., the Amsterdam-registered EV charging group headquartered in Barcelona, has convened its 2026 Annual General Meeting (AGM) for May 22, 2026, in Amsterdam, publishing its 2025 annual report and accounts to shareholders on April 23, 2026. The company has made the full AGM materials, including agenda explanations and financial statements, available free of charge via its investor website and offices.

The AGM agenda includes approval of the 2025 annual accounts, a discharge of directors for their 2025 duties, and the reappointment or appointment of six directors, including CEO Enric Asunción as executive director, with terms running through the 2027 AGM. Shareholders will also vote on renewing the board’s authority to repurchase shares, reappointing EY Accountants as 2026 external auditor, and approving pledges over shares and warrants in key subsidiaries as part of a restructuring, steps that could influence Wallbox’s capital structure and governance flexibility.
